notes: terrible episode must have been done on days of David's spiritual deterioration. The law would not have permitted sons to be put to death for the guilt of a father or a forefather. (Deuteronomy 24:16 is explicit on that; see also Numbers 35:33.) It cannot have been a revelation from the Lord that either required or approved this deed done "to avenge the Gibeonites"-some of whom Saul had slain in spite of the ancient promise of Joshua that they might live in Israel.
